business.name: Elite Electric
business.serviceArea: Bainbridge Island & North Seattle — Ballard, Magnolia, Queen Anne, Fremont, Wallingford, Phinney Ridge, Green Lake
business.about: A licensed master electrician serving Bainbridge Island and the north Seattle neighborhoods. Every permit pulled, every circuit inspected, every job done to code — no gray-area DIY risk, no callbacks.

services.name: Panel Upgrade
services.description: 200A or 400A panel upgrades for older homes being modernized or electrified. Permit pulled, utility coordination handled, load calculation run. The right foundation for EV chargers, heat pumps, and kitchen electrification.

services.name: EV Charger Install
services.description: Level 2 NEMA 14-50 or hardwired EVSE installation with dedicated 50A circuit. Load evaluated before install to confirm your panel can handle it — we don't guess.

services.name: Circuit & Outlet Addition
services.description: New circuits, GFCI/AFCI outlets, USB outlets, outdoor outlets, and dedicated appliance circuits. Permit pulled for anything over minor work.

services.name: Lighting Design & Install
services.description: Recessed lighting, under-cabinet lighting, pendant and fixture installation, and smart-dimmer integration. Wiring done clean, no splice-junction-box shortcuts.

services.name: Emergency Repair
services.description: No power, tripped breakers that won't reset, burning smell, or sparking outlets — we treat these as true emergencies. Licensed tech on-site same day, flat-rate quote before any work starts.

services.name: Whole-Home Rewire
services.description: Full knob-and-tube or aluminum wiring replacement for pre-1970 homes. Phased approach available to keep the home livable throughout. Permit-managed from application through final sign-off.

testimonials.name: Nicole F.
testimonials.text: Lost power to half the house on a Friday night. They answered, diagnosed it over the phone, and had a tech at the door by 9am Saturday. Problem was a failing main breaker — caught before it became a fire.

testimonials.name: Chris A.
testimonials.text: EV charger install in my detached garage — needed a new circuit run from the house panel. They checked the panel load first, pulled the permit, and installed it clean. No exposed conduit, no shortcuts.

testimonials.name: Susan T.
testimonials.text: 1920s house with original knob-and-tube. They rewired the first floor first so we could still live in the house, then finished the second floor the following month. Excellent communication throughout.

testimonials.name: Derek M.
testimonials.text: Panel upgrade went smoothly — they handled all the utility coordination I didn't know was needed. Inspector passed it first visit.

process.title: Assess
process.description: We evaluate your panel capacity, existing wiring condition, and what's needed — in writing, before anything starts. No surprises.

process.title: Permit
process.description: Every job that requires a permit gets one. We handle the application, scheduling, and the inspection sign-off. The city stamp is proof it was done right.

process.title: Install
process.description: Licensed master electrician on every job. Work run clean, circuits labeled, junction boxes accessible. Nothing buried or hidden that shouldn't be.

process.title: Inspect
process.description: Final inspection passed before we close the job. One-year workmanship warranty on all installations. Emergency line active if anything comes up after.

chatbot.greeting: Hi — Elite Electric's coordinator. Emergency, repair, or a new install? I'll get you to the right person fast.
qualifyingQuestions: Is this an emergency — no power, sparking, or burning smell?
If it's a new install, what are you adding — EV charger, panel, outlets, or lighting?
Do you know what panel size you have (100A, 200A)?
chatbot.customInstructions: Lead with urgency for any no-power, sparking, or burning-smell situation — these are emergencies, treat them that way. For new installs, anchor on licensed master electrician credentials, permit management, and load evaluation before committing to work. Reference EV charger expertise and panel upgrade experience for homeowners electrifying.

persona.name: Olivia
persona.title: Electrical Coordinator
persona.proactiveBubbleText: Hi — electrical emergency, repair, or a new install? I can help right now.

faqs.question: Do you handle electrical emergencies?
faqs.answer: Yes — no power, sparking outlets, burning smell, or breakers that won't hold. Same-day service, $175 service call.

faqs.question: Do you install EV chargers?
faqs.answer: Yes — Level 2 EVSE with a dedicated 50A circuit. We check your panel load first to confirm capacity.

faqs.question: Do you pull permits?
faqs.answer: Yes — every job that requires a permit gets one. We handle the application and schedule the inspection.

faqs.question: Are you a licensed master electrician?
faqs.answer: Yes — licensed master electrician in Washington State on every job we take.
